title: Implement MoneyWorks Contact Entity (API-Only)
description: Full backend implementation of the MoneyWorks Contact entity following the established pattern from Product and Account entities. Contacts are a subfile of Names, providing unlimited additional contacts beyond the built-in Contact1/Contact2 fields. Implementation includes canonical types, repository with specialized queries for parent-child relationships, and API controller registration.
why: Enable complete contact management for organizations requiring more than 2 contacts per customer/supplier, supporting complex B2B relationships with role-based contact lookup and communication tracking.

tasks.title: Create Contact enums (MoneyWorksContactRoles)
tasks.description: Create packages/canonical/src/entities/contacts/enums.ts with MoneyWorksContactRoles enum (16-bit flag field). Include bit constants: STATEMENT=1, ORDERS=2, INVOICES=4, REMITTANCES=8, etc. Add helper functions encodeRoles() and decodeRoles() for bit manipulation. Reference: packages/canonical/src/entities/products/enums.ts for pattern.

tasks.title: Create Contact TypeScript types/interfaces
tasks.description: Create packages/canonical/src/entities/contacts/types.ts with MoneyWorksContact interface (all MW Contact table fields: Name, Email, Phone, ParentSeq, Role, etc.), MoneyWorksContactCreateInput (required fields), MoneyWorksContactUpdateInput (optional fields), and MoneyWorksContactFilter. Use @moneyworks-dsl PURE annotations. Reference: packages/canonical/src/entities/products/types.ts

tasks.title: Create Contact canonical index exports
tasks.description: Create packages/canonical/src/entities/contacts/index.ts that exports all from ./enums and ./types. Update packages/canonical/src/index.ts to add 'export * as Contacts from ./entities/contacts' and update CANONICAL_VERSION.entities.contacts status to 'complete'. Reference: packages/canonical/src/entities/products/index.ts

tasks.title: Create ContactRepository with postProcess/prepare
tasks.description: Create packages/data/src/repositories/contact.repository.ts extending BaseMoneyWorksRepository<MoneyWorksContact, MoneyWorksContactCreateInput, MoneyWorksContactUpdateInput>. Set tableName='Contact', primaryKey='SequenceNumber'. Implement postProcess() for numeric fields (ParentSeq, Role, Colour) and boolean parsing. Implement prepare() for MW format conversion. Reference: packages/data/src/repositories/product.repository.ts

tasks.title: Add specialized query methods to ContactRepository
tasks.description: Add to contact.repository.ts: findByParentSeq(seq: number) - find all contacts for a Name record, findByNameCode(nameCode: string) - lookup Name.SequenceNumber then query Contact.ParentSeq, findByRole(roleBit: number) - bitwise role filter, findByEmail(email: string) - email lookup. Use MWScript expressions where possible. Reference: product.repository.ts findByCode, findByType patterns.

tasks.title: Update data package exports for ContactRepository
tasks.description: Update packages/data/src/repositories/index.ts to export ContactRepository and MoneyWorksContact type. Update packages/data/src/index.ts to export ContactRepository and add to createMoneyWorksClient/createDataLayer repositories object. Follow triple-export pattern. Reference: existing Product exports in both files.

tasks.title: Create ContactController extending BaseTableController
tasks.description: Create packages/api/src/controllers/contact.ts with ContactController class extending BaseTableController. Set tableName='Contact', displayName='Contacts', description='Additional contacts linked to Names'. Create ContactRepository instance. Implement getPrimaryKey() returning 'SequenceNumber'. Add validateTableSpecific() with filter validation. Reference: packages/api/src/controllers/product.ts

tasks.title: Register ContactController in TableRegistry
tasks.description: Update packages/api/src/registry/table-registry.ts: import ContactController, add this.register(new ContactController(this.client)) in registerTables(). Contact will appear in available tables list. Reference: existing Product, Account registrations in same file.

tasks.title: Run typecheck and verify all AC
tasks.description: Run 'bun run typecheck' from repository root. Verify zero TypeScript errors. Manually verify: 1) Contact types exported from @moneyworks/canonical, 2) ContactRepository exported from @moneyworks/data, 3) Contact appears in TableRegistry. Document any issues found.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Canonical types defined in packages/canonical/src/entities/contacts/ with MoneyWorksContact interface, MoneyWorksContactRoles enum, and ContactCode branded type
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: Files created: enums.ts (9455 bytes), types.ts (7345 bytes), index.ts (101 bytes). MoneyWorksContact interface and MoneyWorksContactRoles enum verified present.

acceptanceCriteria.description: ContactRepository extends BaseMoneyWorksRepository with postProcess() for numeric/boolean parsing and prepare() for MW format conversion
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: contact.repository.ts created with postProcess() parsing ParentSeq, SequenceNumber, Order, Role, Colour, UserNum fields. prepare() method handles all contact fields for MW format. Exported from data/src/index.ts and repositories/index.ts.

acceptanceCriteria.description: ContactRepository includes specialized query methods: findByParentSeq(), findByNameCode(), findByRole(), findByEmail()
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: All 4 methods implemented: findByParentSeq(parentSeq), findByNameCode(nameCode) with Name lookup, findByRole(roleBit) with bitwise search, findByEmail(email). Also added findByParentSeqAndRole() and searchByName() bonus methods.

acceptanceCriteria.description: ContactController extends BaseTableController and is registered in TableRegistry - Contact appears in GET /api/v1/tables 'available' list
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: ContactController created in api/src/controllers/contact.ts with tableName='Contact', displayName='Contacts'. Registered in table-registry.ts: this.register(new ContactController(this.client)).

acceptanceCriteria.description: TypeScript compilation passes with zero errors (bun run typecheck)
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: packages/canonical and packages/data compile with zero errors. packages/api has pre-existing unrelated errors (CacheService type mismatch in app.ts). Contact-specific code compiles cleanly.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Role bit-mapping utilities available for encoding/decoding contact roles (16-bit field with legacy Contact1/Contact2 support)
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: enums.ts contains: encodeRoles(), decodeRoles(), hasRole(), addRole(), removeRole(), toMoneyWorksHex(), fromMoneyWorksHex(), getRoleNames(). Also MoneyWorksLegacyContactRoles enum with CONTACT_1_ASSUMED (0x10000) and CONTACT_2_ASSUMED (0x20000).

decisions.title: Use SequenceNumber as primary key instead of Code
decisions.description: Contacts use SequenceNumber as primary key (like Names) rather than Code (like Products). This aligns with MoneyWorks Contact table structure where contacts don't have a unique code field.
decisions.rationale: Contact table in MoneyWorks uses auto-generated SequenceNumber, unlike Product which has user-defined Code.
alternatives: Use ParentSeq + Order as composite key

decisions.title: Include legacy Contact1/Contact2 role bits in enums
decisions.description: Added MoneyWorksLegacyContactRoles enum with 0x10000 and 0x20000 bit values for accessing legacy Contact1/Contact2 fields via GetContactForRole function.
decisions.rationale: MoneyWorks uses these extended bits for backward compatibility with built-in contact fields in Names table.

learnings.description: Contact eMail field is 63 chars max - SHORTER than Names.email (80 chars). Must validate/truncate when migrating contacts from Names embedded fields to Contact subfile.
learnings.impact: Data migration consideration for Contact.eMail field

learnings.description: MoneyWorks search supports bitwise operations: (Role&#xx)!=0 for role-based filtering. This enables efficient role queries without fetching all records.
learnings.impact: Performance optimization for role-based contact queries
